revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24598347618|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Akanabe-jinja]][[ja:茜部神社]] {{Expand Japanese|茜部神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Akanabe Shrine | native_name = 茜部神社 | image = Akanabe jinja2008-1.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Akanabe Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.374946|136.751698}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Ōjin|ar|الإمبراطور أوجين|arz|الامبراطور اوجين|azb|اوجین (ایمپیراتور)|bn|সম্রাট ওজিন|cs|Ódžin|de|Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|es|Ōjin Tennō|eu|Ōjin enperadorea|fa|امپراتور اوجین|fi|Ōjin|fr|Ōjin|hr|Ōjin|id|Kaisar Ōjin|it|Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|ka|ოძინი|ko|오진 천황|nl|Ojin|pl|Ōjin|pt|Ojin|ru|Император Одзин|sh|Car Ōjin|simple|Emperor Ōjin|sl|Cesar Odžin|sv|Ojin|th|จักรพรรดิโอจิง|tl|Emperador Ōjin|tr|Ōjin (imparator)|uk|Імператор Одзін|uz|Imperator Ōjin|vi|Thiên hoàng Ōjin|yo|Emperor Ōjin|zh_min_nan|Ôzin Thian-hông|zh_yue|應神天皇|zh|应神天皇|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} }} {{nihongo|'''Akanabe Shrine'''|茜部神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mino Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mino Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mino Province|ja|美濃国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11609223|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mino Province}} ** Series ordinal: 19 ** P155: {{ill|Q135039903|ltq=Q135039903|qid=Q135039903|1=Hinamorino Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Inaba Shrine|lt=Inaba Shrine|en|Inaba Shrine|eu|Inaba santutegia|fr|Inaba-jinja|ja|伊奈波神社|ko|이나바 신사|th|ศาลเจ้าอินาบะ|zh|伊奈波神社|qid=Q701414|1=Inaba Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q45798|ltq=Q45798|qid=Q45798|1=Gifu}} * {{ill|Mino Province|lt=Mino Province|ckb|پارێزگای مینۆ|cs|Mino (provincie)|de|Provinz Mino|en|Mino Province|es|Provincia de Mino|eu|Mino probintzia|fa|ولایت مینو|fr|Province de Mino|gl|Provincia de Mino|hak|Mî-nùng-koet|id|Provinsi Mino|it|Mino (provincia)|ja|美濃国|ko|미노국|lt|Mino žemė|nl|Mino (provincie)|pt|Província de Mino|ru|Мино (провинция)|simple|Mino Province|sk|Mino (provincia)|sr|Провинција Мино|th|แคว้นมิโนะ|tr|Mino (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Міно|vi|Tỉnh Mino|wuu|美濃国|zh_min_nan|Mino-kok|zh_yue|美濃國|zh|美濃國|qid=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} * {{ill|Atsumi district|lt=Atsumi district|ja|厚見郡|zh|厚見郡|qid=Q11409326|1=Atsumi district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.374946|136.751698}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == image (P18) == * Akanabe jinja2008-1.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q317997|ltq=Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181941 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == VIAF cluster ID (P214) == * 255513008 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8200005000224 == NDL Authority ID (P349) == * 00629798 == Commons category (P373) == * Akanabe-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県岐阜市茜部寺屋敷3丁目135番地', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Akanabe-jinja}} [[JA:茜部神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11617261}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Akanabe Shrine |Image = [[Image:Akanabe jinja2008-1.jpg|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gifu City|Q45798|qid=Q45798|1=Gifu}} 3-135 Akanabe Terayashiki |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}
{{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|qid=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Gōsha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |Founded = {{ill|lt=830|Q30456|qid=Q30456|1=830}} (7th year of {{ill|lt=Tenchō|Q1373138|qid=Q1373138|1=Tenchō}}) |Main hall = |Other names = |Annual festival = April 5 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Akanabe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Akanabe Shrine''' (あかなべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu City|Q45798|qid=Q45798|1=Gifu}}, Japan. It is listed as a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a gōsha. It is regarded as the tutelary shrine (ujigami) of the Akanabe area of Gifu City. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (ほむたわけのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} (おきながたらしひめのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|qid=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} (たけうちすくね) : Until {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|qid=Q7717|1=1868}} (the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}), {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|qid=Q22070227|lt=Hime Ōkami|1=Himegami}} was enshrined. === History === During the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|qid=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, this area was the Tōdai-ji estate known as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Atsumi Estate|1=UNKNOWN}} (later Akanabe Estate). Akanabe Shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=830|Q30456|qid=Q30456|1=830}} (7th year of {{ill|lt=Tenchō|Q1373138|qid=Q1373138|1=Tenchō}}) by the transfer (kanjō) of the deity from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Usa Hachiman Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is considered one of the earlier shrines established as a branch of Usa Hachiman Shrine. The original enshrined deities were {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|lt=Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}, and {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|qid=Q22070227|lt=Hime Ōkami|1=Himegami}}. Later, in {{ill|lt=833|Q30377|qid=Q30377|1=833}} (10th year of Tenchō), {{ill|lt=Kūkai|Q222227|qid=Q222227|1=Kūkai}} established Jōju-in as a {{ill|lt=Jingū-ji|Q1074971|qid=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} (temple associated with the shrine). During the {{ill|lt=Nanboku-chō period (Japan)|Q264012|qid=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}} and the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, the shrine was regarded as the guardian deity of {{ill|lt=Kawate Castle|Q2969463|qid=Q2969463|1=Kawate Castle}}, {{ill|lt=Kami-Akanabe Castle|Q17226097|qid=Q17226097|1=Kamiakanabe Castle}}, and {{ill|lt=Kanō Castle#Early Kanō Castle|Q2969458|qid=Q2969458|lt=Kanō Castle|1=Kanō Castle}}, and was protected by the {{ill|lt=Toki clan|Q687635|qid=Q687635|1=Toki clan}} and the {{ill|lt=Saitō clan|Q2975353|qid=Q2975353|1=Saitō clan}}. However, after the death of {{ill|lt=Saitō Dōsan|Q1202670|qid=Q1202670|1=Saitō Dōsan}}, it was destroyed by fire in warfare in {{ill|lt=1556|Q6590|qid=Q6590|1=1556}} (2nd year of {{ill|lt=Kōji (Japan)|Q1143910|qid=Q1143910|lt=Kōji|1=Kōji (Muromachi period)}}). It was later rebuilt and came under the protection of the {{ill|lt=Oda clan|Q1132077|qid=Q1132077|1=Oda clan}}. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was protected by successive lords of {{ill|lt=Kanō Castle|Q2969458|qid=Q2969458|1=Kanō Castle}}. In the early {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|qid=Q3125096}}, due to the {{ill|lt=Haibutsu kishaku|Q702298|qid=Q702298|1=Haibutsu kishaku}} (abolition of Buddhism and destruction of Buddhist images), Jōju-in was abolished. In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|qid=Q7717|1=1868}} (the first year of Meiji), one of the enshrined deities, Hime Ōkami, was replaced with Takenouchi no Sukune. The annual festival was originally held on October 15, but in {{ill|lt=1950|Q18597|qid=Q18597|1=1950}} (25th year of {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}) it was changed to April 5. This change was made to coincide with the {{ill|lt=Gifu Festival|Q11471288|qid=Q11471288|1=Gifu Matsuri}} (the annual festival of {{ill|lt=Inaba Shrine|Q701414|qid=Q701414|1=Inaba Shrine}}). === Cultural properties === * Stone komainu (guardian lion-dog statues) – designated cultural property of Gifu City === On-site information === ;Address * {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Gifu City|Q45798|qid=Q45798|1=Gifu}} 3-135 Akanabe Terayashiki ;Access * {{ill|lt=Gifu Bus|Q5560101|qid=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Jōgō Bus|1=Gifu Bus}} Akanabe–Mitahora Line: get off at “Terayashiki” bus stop, 3-minute walk ** From {{ill|lt=JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal|Q11226229|qid=Q11226229|1=JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal}}, platform 4, bound for “Shimosaba,” “Takakuwa,” or “Prefectural Automobile Hall” ** From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal|Q11415771|qid=Q11415771|1=Meitetsu Gifu Bus Terminal}}, platform 2, bound for “Shimosaba,” “Takakuwa,” or “Prefectural Automobile Hall” * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|qid=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}} {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Takehana Line|Q901442|qid=Q901442|lt=Takehana Line|1=Meitetsu Takehana Line}}, about a 12-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Nishi-Kasamatsu Station|Q7040360|qid=Q7040360|1=Nishi Kasamatsu Station}} === External links === * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=971 Akanabe Shrine – Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{Authority control}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Akanabe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Akanabe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Akanabe Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]]
